era_context: Late Bronze Age II. The world's major powers remain stable. Egypt, the Hittites, Mycenae, and the Shang Dynasty are all at or near their height. International trade networks are robust. This is approximately 200 years before the Late Bronze Age collapse.

events.title: Ramesses II — reign continues with military and administrative activities
events.description: Ramesses II continues his long reign. Egyptian power is at its height. Military campaigns, building projects, and administrative activities continue. The pharaoh maintains control over a vast empire stretching from Nubia to Syria.

sources.name: Egyptian temple inscriptions and administrative papyri

events.certainty_note: Ramesses II's reign is well-documented; this year falls within it with high confidence.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: Documentation for this year is minimal. The historical record is almost entirely dependent on Egyptian and Hittite inscriptions. Most of the world's population and regions left no written record. The apparent 'stability' of this period is an artefact of the survival of elite records.
